it.name: Itala
it.description: All or majority of Old Latin witnesses

latt.name: Latin tradition
latt.description: Entire Latin tradition supporting the same Greek reading

lat(t).name: Latin tradition (with exceptions)
lat(t).description: Entire Latin tradition with a few exceptions due to intraversional differences

lat.name: Latin (partial)
lat.description: Vulgate and part of the Old Latin tradition

vg.name: Vulgata
vg.description: Agreement of most important Vulgate editions

vg_st.name: Vulgata Stuttgartiensis
vg_st.description: Biblia sacra iuxta Vulgatam versionem, Stuttgart 1994

sy_s.name: Sinaitic Syriac
sy_s.description: Syriac Sinaiticus (Codex Sinaiticus Syriacus), 4th/5th century palimpsest

sy_p.name: Peshitta
sy_p.description: Syriac Peshitta, standard Syriac NT, 5th century

sy_h.name: Harclean Syriac
sy_h.description: Syriac Harclensis, revised by Thomas of Harkel, 616 AD

sa.name: Sahidic Coptic
sa.description: Coptic version in Sahidic dialect (Upper Egypt)

bo.name: Bohairic Coptic
bo.description: Coptic version in Bohairic dialect (Lower Egypt)

mae.name: Middle Egyptian Coptic
mae.description: Coptic version in Middle Egyptian dialect

f1.name: Family 1
f1.description: Group of minuscule manuscripts (1, 118, 131, 209, etc.) sharing common textual features

f13.name: Family 13 (Ferrar group)
f13.description: Group of minuscule manuscripts (13, 69, 124, 174, 230, etc.) sharing common textual features

M.name: Majority Text
M.description: The reading of the majority of all manuscripts (Byzantine text-type)

txt.name: Text
txt.description: The reading adopted in the main text of NA28

*.name: Original scribe
*.description: The original hand of the manuscript (before corrections)

c.name: Corrector
c.description: A corrector (when specific corrector cannot be identified)

vid.name: Videtur (apparently)
vid.description: The reading appears to be as cited, but cannot be verified with certainty

supp.name: Supplementum
supp.description: A later supplement replacing a lost portion of the manuscript

mg.name: Margin
mg.description: Reading found in the margin of the manuscript

Or.name: Origen
Or.description: Church father Origen of Alexandria (c. 185-254 AD)

Eus.name: Eusebius
Eus.description: Church father Eusebius of Caesarea (c. 260-340 AD)

Did.name: Didymus
Did.description: Didymus the Blind of Alexandria (c. 313-398 AD)

Cyp.name: Cyprian
Cyp.description: Church father Cyprian of Carthage (c. 200-258 AD)

33.name: Minuscule 33
33.description: Called 'Queen of the cursives' — high-quality Alexandrian text
